steps.title: Find the two independent clauses that need coordination
steps.content:
The sentence contains two independent clauses with the same subject (The resulting course): one about length (was 2,500 miles long) and one about representation (modeled a vision of the EU's unifying aims).

The blank must connect these clauses while incorporating the detail about crossing borders.

steps.title: Apply the coordination rule
steps.content:
Option D (and, crossing) correctly coordinates the two independent clauses with and, inserting the participial phrase crossing seven separate borders of neighboring countries between the connector and the second verb modeled to create the pattern [IC], and [participial phrase], [IC], which is Standard English; A, B, and C mishandle this structure through punctuation (A), tense issues (B), or leaving “modeled” uncoordinated (C).

That matches choice D — D is correct.
